Redesign momentum-space HF, unify naming and docs

- Redesign interaction preprocessing in hartreefock_momentum.jl:
  merge build_Wr/build_Vr into unified build_Vr, add build_Vk stub,
  remove _symmetrize_interactions, update build_heff_k! signature and
  downstream callers (build_Tk, _run_scf_k, solve_hfk)
- Rename build_T_matrix → build_T, build_U_matrix → build_U in
  hartreefock_real.jl; update exports and test accordingly
- Unify docs structure: H1 title / H2 Theory|Code / H3 numbered sections;
  replace function-name headings with descriptive physical content titles
- Rewrite hartreefock_real.md theory in explicit ia,jb,kc,ld notation
  matching momentum-space doc; expand SCF section with 8-step walkthrough

  G update (T=0 and finite-T), convergence criterion, DIIS/linear mixing,
  multiple restarts, and output fields

commit.message:
Fix PWA session persistence — add Max-Age to session_token cookies

Neon Auth's upstream Better Auth server sets session_token as a session
cookie (no Max-Age/Expires), which gets cleared whenever a standalone
PWA is closed. This wraps both the auth API handler and proxy middleware
to inject Max-Age=7days on session_token cookies, so sessions survive
app restarts without requiring re-login.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use importlib.util to load generate_risk_data — fixes ModuleNotFoundError on Railway

Root cause identified via /api/health: ModuleNotFoundError for generate_risk_data
because Railway's Python sys.path did not include the script directory when
running create_sqlite_db.py.

Fix: replace bare 'import generate_risk_data' with importlib.util.spec_from_file_location
using an explicit absolute path built from __file__. This loads the module directly from
the filesystem regardless of sys.path configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Supabase 조인 타입 캐스팅 빌드 에러 수정 + 퀴즈 시스템 개선

- traineeLink `as unknown as` 패턴으로 TypeScript 빌드 에러 해결
- 온보딩 퀴즈 시스템 관련 변경 반영

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: ~90% faster analysis via parallel execution + caching

Translation (the main bottleneck) now runs all batches simultaneously:
  Before: 10 batches × ~20s each = ~200s (sequential)
  After:  10 batches all at once  =  ~20s (parallel, ThreadPoolExecutor)

Additional wins:
- Translation and analysis now run concurrently (2-worker pool in endpoint)
  so the ~15s analysis call overlaps with translation instead of adding to it
- BATCH_SIZE 50 → 100: halves number of API round-trips
- In-memory cache (last 60 videos): repeat analyses return instantly
- analyze_transcript accepts raw subtitles directly so it fires immediately
  without waiting for translation to finish

Expected total time: ~20-30s (was 4-5 min) = ~88% reduction

This resolves the "Error error from registry: denied" when pulling images from ghcr.io

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

M0-M9: Complete multi-agent AI system for GBM trial outcome prediction

Major Components:
- M0: Project scaffolding, dependencies, and API connectivity verification
- M1: SQLAlchemy ORM with SQLite backend, API clients with retry logic, disk caching
- M2: ClinicalTrials.gov data collection (768 Phase II/III GBM trials, 2010-2025)
  * Fixed: SQLite Date type handling (Python date objects instead of strings)
  * Query: 4 search terms, phase filtering via filter.advanced, pagination support
- M3: TCGA genomic data pipeline and drug properties collection
  * TCGA: 20,513 genes with expression/mutation data from cBioPortal datahub
  * Fixed: Git LFS file access via media.githubusercontent.com/media/ URLs
  * Drugs: 817 unique drugs, 303 with PubChem properties, BBB classification
  * Mapping: 302 trials linked to target genes (MGMT top target: 137 trials)
- M4: Agent infrastructure with Pydantic v2 structured outputs and caching
  * BaseAgent abstract class with JSON parsing, LOOCV support, DB persistence
  * Output schemas: BaseAgentOutput, DesignAnalysisOutput, BiologyAnalysisOutput,
    BBBAnalysisOutput, PrecedentAnalysisOutput, CSOSynthesisOutput (0-100 risk scores)
- M5: Four specialist domain agents
  * TrialDesignAgent: Power analysis, endpoint appropriateness, sample size
  * TargetBiologyAgent: TCGA expression, pathway redundancy, GBM heterogeneity
  * BBBAgent: Physicochemical properties, P-gp efflux, delivery mechanisms
  * PrecedentAgent: Historical trial patterns with LOOCV trial exclusion
- M6: CSO synthesis agent and orchestration system
  * CSOAgent: Weighs domain expert outputs, identifies failure modes
  * TrialAnalysisSystem: Orchestrates all 5 agents in parallel with timeout handling
  * ReportGenerator: Markdown reports and batch summary tables
- M7: Baseline predictors for comparison
  * NaivePredictor: 80.0 base rate constant
  * RuleBasedPredictor: 15+ heuristics (sample size, design, BBB, phase, target)
  * SingleAgentPredictor: Ablation without specialist agents
- M8: Evaluation framework with comprehensive metrics
  * compute_all_metrics(): AUC-ROC, F1, AUC-PR, MCC, Brier, ECE, sensitivity, specificity
  * bootstrap_confidence_intervals(): 1000 iterations, 95% CI
  * find_optimal_threshold(): Youden's J statistic
  * delong_test(): Statistical comparison of AUC curves
- M9: Publication-quality visualizations (300 DPI)
  * ROC curves, Precision-Recall curves, calibration plots
  * Risk score distributions, domain score heatmaps, confusion matrices

Automated Trial Outcome Classification:
- Stage 1: Rule-based from termination reasons (9 trials)
- Stage 2: Results-based parsing of p-values and efficacy measures (45 trials)
- Stage 3: LLM-assisted classification for ambiguous cases (65 trials, $0.49 cost)
- Total: 123 classified trials (112 FAILURE, 11 SUCCESS)
- Fixed: Hazard ratio direction checking (HR > 1 = worse for survival outcomes)
- Integration: Results saved to database and review CSV

API Client Improvements:
- Fixed: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY loading from .env file
- Updated: AnthropicClientWrapper to explicitly pass api_key parameter

Testing:
- 57 tests covering connectivity, database, agents, data collection, evaluation
- All tests passing with comprehensive fixtures and mock data

Ready for M10: Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation evaluation with 123 labeled trials

Fix login: set cookie on NextResponse object directly

The cookies() API from next/headers doesn't reliably attach
Set-Cookie headers when a Route Handler returns its own
NextResponse. Switched to response.cookies.set() pattern.
Also added router.refresh() before redirect to clear cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix infinite loop and missing data attributes on environment buttons

Fixed two critical issues:
1. Infinite recursion: Removed click handler that called $(this).click() on itself
2. Missing data attributes: Fixed case sensitivity - changed 'data-environmentVariable'
   to 'data-environmentvariable' (lowercase) and added .toLowerCase() to key names

The environment switcher expects lowercase attribute names like
'data-environmentvariabledisplayname', not camelCase.

Errors fixed:
- "Maximum call stack size exceeded" (infinite loop)
- "Fix the env variable displayname/clientid/etc" (missing/wrong attributes)

commit.message:
CI/CD improvements for 1.0.0 release (#3)

* fix: use hardcoded repo name in workflow conditions

GitHub Actions env variables defined at workflow level are not
accessible in if/condition expressions. Use the hardcoded
repository name directly.

Fixes CI validation errors.

* Prepare for 1.0.0 release

- Update Go from 1.22 to 1.23
- Remove GOPROXY: direct (use default Go proxy)
- Remove enableCrossOsArchive (unnecessary)
- Skip build job except on release tags
- Replace third-party release action with official gh CLI
- Remove coverage reporting (Codecov)
- Add windows/arm64 build target
- Package binaries in tar.gz/zip archives with simple naming
- Generate release notes from commits, filtering test/CI/Claude changes
